title: weinmannia dichotoma var. monticola
intro: Weinmannia dichotoma var. monticola, also known as the mountain tukoro, is a variety of the Weinmannia dichotoma tree native to New Zealand. It has been used in traditional Maori medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The leaves and bark of the mountain tukoro have been used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, offering health benefits such as reduced inflammation and improved immune function. In herbal preparations, the mountain tukoro is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, allowing for the extraction and utilization of its medicinal properties.
